description_long: This is a two-story brick house with a steeply pitched gable roof and four prominent brick chimneys, two on each side. The facade features symmetrical window placement with tall, narrow sash windows framed by stone lintels and sills. The main entryway is sheltered by a covered porch supported by simple posts, with a small set of steps leading up to the door. The roof is clad in dark slate or shingle material, and the brickwork is accented by stone trim along the roofline and around the windows. Some subtle vegetation is visible at the base of the structure, suggesting a slightly aged or established residence. The overall design is reminiscent of Victorian or Gothic Revival architecture, with its steep rooflines and decorative masonry.
description: A two-story brick house with a steep gable roof, four tall chimneys, symmetrical windows, and a covered front porch with stone and wood accents.
description_view_0: Rear view showing two large chimneys, the steep gable roof, and a central window between the chimney stacks.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the main entry porch, two upper windows, and the prominent central gable with a tall chimney.
description_view_2: Opposite rear-side view with a smaller gable, two windows, and the covered porch extending from the side.
description_view_3: Front angled view showing the symmetrical window arrangement, central gable, and all four chimneys.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with steps, simple posts, and a central door beneath the main gable
